channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic $t–{t}^{′}–J$ model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level $δ$ and hopping ratio ${t}^{′}/t$, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic t–t′–J model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level δ and hopping ratio t′/t, we uncover six d…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.description:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.dc:creator: Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Localization Transition for Interacting Quantum Particles in Colored-Noise Disorder
item.description:
Author(s): Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi

We investigate the localization transition of interacting particles in a one-dimensional system with colored-noise disorder, where backward scattering processes are suppressed beyond a cutoff. Employing two complementary renormalization group procedures, we derive the phase diagram and reveal a sign…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096504] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi

We investigate the localization transition of interacting particles in a one-dimensional system with colored-noise disorder, where backward scattering processes are suppressed beyond a cutoff. Employing two complementary renormalization group procedures, we derive the phase diagram and reveal a sign…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096504] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Localization Transition for Interacting Quantum Particles in Colored-Noise Disorder
item.dc:creator: Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Pressure-Induced 18 K Superconductivity and Two Superconducting Phases in ${\text{CuIr}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding

CuIr2S4 becomes a bulk superconductor with Tc of 18.2 K under pressure, a new record for spinels, and splits into two distinct superconducting phases that coexist over a broad window.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096505] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding

CuIr2S4 becomes a bulk superconductor with Tc of 18.2 K under pressure, a new record for spinels, and splits into two distinct superconducting phases that coexist over a broad window.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096505] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-Induced 18 K Superconductivity and Two Superconducting Phases in ${\text{CuIr}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Universal Chern Model on Arbitrary Triangulations
item.description:
Author(s): Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan

Given a triangulation of a closed orientable surface, we place single-mode resonators or single-orbital artificial atoms at its vertices, edges, and facets, and we devise near-neighbor hopping terms derived from the boundary and Poincaré duality maps of the simplicial complex of the triangulation. R…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096602] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan

Given a triangulation of a closed orientable surface, we place single-mode resonators or single-orbital artificial atoms at its vertices, edges, and facets, and we devise near-neighbor hopping terms derived from the boundary and Poincaré duality maps of the simplicial complex of the triangulation. R…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096602] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Chern Model on Arbitrary Triangulations
item.dc:creator: Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Oscillations of Nonlinear Electrical Transport in a Topological Dirac Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Vijaysankar Kalappattil, Chuanpu Liu, Zhijie Chen, Vipul Sharma, Kai Liu, Jinke Tang, Steven S.-L. Zhang, and Mingzhong Wu

Quantum oscillations in nonlinear electrical transport reveal the geometry and spin orientation of the Fermi surface in the Dirac semimetal α-Sn.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096603] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vijaysankar Kalappattil, Chuanpu Liu, Zhijie Chen, Vipul Sharma, Kai Liu, Jinke Tang, Steven S.-L. Zhang, and Mingzhong Wu

Quantum oscillations in nonlinear electrical transport reveal the geometry and spin orientation of the Fermi surface in the Dirac semimetal α-Sn.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096603] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Oscillations of Nonlinear Electrical Transport in a Topological Dirac Semimetal
item.dc:creator: Vijaysankar Kalappattil, Chuanpu Liu, Zhijie Chen, Vipul Sharma, Kai Liu, Jinke Tang, Steven S.-L. Zhang, and Mingzhong Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Diameter-Controlled High-Order Vortex States and Magnon Hybridization in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ Nanotubes
item.description:
Author(s): Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu

Curved magnets offer a rich phase diagram and hold great promise for next-generation spintronic technologies. This Letter establishes the paramount significance of high-order vortex states (e.g., $3φ$ with winding number $n≥2$) in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ nanotubes, which uniquely enable magnonic functi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096703] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu

Curved magnets offer a rich phase diagram and hold great promise for next-generation spintronic technologies. This Letter establishes the paramount significance of high-order vortex states (e.g., 3φ with winding number n≥2) in VSe2 nanotubes, which uniquely enable magnonic functionalities fundamenta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096703] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Diameter-Controlled High-Order Vortex States and Magnon Hybridization in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ Nanotubes
item.dc:creator: Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anderson Localization in a Two-Dimensional Metal
item.description:
Author(s): Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

A new layered material enabled researchers to document a dramatic change in metallic electron behavior as the material goes from 3D to 2D.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096401] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

A new layered material enabled researchers to document a dramatic change in metallic electron behavior as the material goes from 3D to 2D.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096401] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Anderson Localization in a Two-Dimensional Metal
item.dc:creator: Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.description:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.dc:creator: Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Paired Parton Trial States for the Superfluid-Fractional Chern Insulator Transition
item.description:
Author(s): Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon

We consider a model of hard-core bosons on a lattice half-filling a Chern band such that the system has a continuous transition between a fractional Chern insulator (FCI) and a superfluid state (SF) depending on the bandwidth to bandspacing ratio. We construct a parton-inspired trial wave function A…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096601] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon

We consider a model of hard-core bosons on a lattice half-filling a Chern band such that the system has a continuous transition between a fractional Chern insulator (FCI) and a superfluid state (SF) depending on the bandwidth to bandspacing ratio. We construct a parton-inspired trial wave function A…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096601] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Paired Parton Trial States for the Superfluid-Fractional Chern Insulator Transition
item.dc:creator: Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the $5d$ iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the $1/3$-hole self-doped system ${…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the 5d iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the 1/3-hole self-doped system La3Ir3…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$
item.dc:creator: Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetic Signature of Chiral Phonons Revealed by Neutron Spectroscopy in Ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{1.75}{\mathrm{Zn}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$
item.description:
Author(s): Song Bao, Junbo Liao, Zhentao Huang, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhen Ma, Bo Zhang, Shufan Cheng, Hao Xu, Zihang Song, Shuai Dong, Maofeng Wu, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Mitsutaka Nakamura, Tom Fennell, Dmitry Khalyavin, and Jinsheng Wen

Neutron scattering has provided a new and broader view of the twirling collective atomic vibrations in a magnetic crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Song Bao, Junbo Liao, Zhentao Huang, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhen Ma, Bo Zhang, Shufan Cheng, Hao Xu, Zihang Song, Shuai Dong, Maofeng Wu, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Mitsutaka Nakamura, Tom Fennell, Dmitry Khalyavin, and Jinsheng Wen

Neutron scattering has provided a new and broader view of the twirling collective atomic vibrations in a magnetic crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic Signature of Chiral Phonons Revealed by Neutron Spectroscopy in Ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{1.75}{\mathrm{Zn}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$
item.dc:creator: Song Bao, Junbo Liao, Zhentao Huang, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhen Ma, Bo Zhang, Shufan Cheng, Hao Xu, Zihang Song, Shuai Dong, Maofeng Wu, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Mitsutaka Nakamura, Tom Fennell, Dmitry Khalyavin, and Jinsheng Wen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantization of Spin Circular Photogalvanic Effect in Altermagnetic Weyl Semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami

We theoretically predict a spin-current analog of the quantized circular photogalvanic effect in Weyl semimetals. This phenomenon is forbidden in antiferromagnets by symmetry but uniquely allowed in altermagnets, highlighting a novel and intrinsic characteristic of altermagnetism. To systematically …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096701] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami

We theoretically predict a spin-current analog of the quantized circular photogalvanic effect in Weyl semimetals. This phenomenon is forbidden in antiferromagnets by symmetry but uniquely allowed in altermagnets, highlighting a novel and intrinsic characteristic of altermagnetism. To systematically …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096701] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantization of Spin Circular Photogalvanic Effect in Altermagnetic Weyl Semimetals
item.dc:creator: Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Deterministic Switching of the Néel Vector by Asymmetric Spin Torque
item.description:
Author(s): Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao

Néel vector, the order parameter of collinear antiferromagnets, serves as a state variable in associated antiferromagnetic (AFM) spintronic devices to encode information. A deterministic switching of Néel vector is crucial for the write-in operation, which, however, remains a challenging problem in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096702] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao

Néel vector, the order parameter of collinear antiferromagnets, serves as a state variable in associated antiferromagnetic (AFM) spintronic devices to encode information. A deterministic switching of Néel vector is crucial for the write-in operation, which, however, remains a challenging problem in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096702] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Deterministic Switching of the Néel Vector by Asymmetric Spin Torque
item.dc:creator: Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ultrafast One-Dimensional Peierls-Distortion Dynamics in $1{\mathrm{T}}^{′}\text{−}\mathrm{Re}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ Revealed by 4D Electron Microscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu

Rhenium disulfide (${\mathrm{ReS}}_{2}$), a prototypical 2D semiconductor with an anisotropic ${1\mathrm{T}}^{′}$ structure due to the pronounced Peierls distortion, has demonstrated great potential for polarization-dependent optoelectronics and photonics. Here, we report an ultrafast phase transiti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096901] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu

Rhenium disulfide (ReS2), a prototypical 2D semiconductor with an anisotropic 1T′ structure due to the pronounced Peierls distortion, has demonstrated great potential for polarization-dependent optoelectronics and photonics. Here, we report an ultrafast phase transition occurring within 1 ps, accomp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096901] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast One-Dimensional Peierls-Distortion Dynamics in $1{\mathrm{T}}^{′}\text{−}\mathrm{Re}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ Revealed by 4D Electron Microscopy
item.dc:creator: Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlinear Terahertz Electroluminescence from Dirac-Landau Polaritons
item.description:
Author(s): B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe

We report Dirac–Landau polaritons observed by terahertz (THz) magnetoreflectivity spectroscopy, demonstrating strong coupling between cyclotron transitions of two-dimensional Dirac fermions in HgTe quantum wells and optical cavity modes. Under pulsed electrical injection we observe efficient nonline…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096902] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe

We report Dirac–Landau polaritons observed by terahertz (THz) magnetoreflectivity spectroscopy, demonstrating strong coupling between cyclotron transitions of two-dimensional Dirac fermions in HgTe quantum wells and optical cavity modes. Under pulsed electrical injection we observe efficient nonline…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096902] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlinear Terahertz Electroluminescence from Dirac-Landau Polaritons
item.dc:creator: B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Single-Atom Magnets on Thermally Stable Adsorption Sites: Dy on NaCl(100)
item.description:
Author(s): M. Pivetta, M. Blanco-Rey, S. Reynaud, R. Baltic, A. Rary-Zinque, S. Toda Cosi, F. Patthey, B. V. Sorokin, A. Singha, F. Donati, A. Barla, L. Persichetti, P. Gambardella, A. Arnau, F. Delgado, S. Rusponi, and H. Brune

We report magnetic bistability in single Dy atoms on NaCl(100) thin films. Individual Dy atoms substituting Na at the surface of the NaCl layer are thermally stable up to at least 300 K, display $4{f}^{9}$ occupancy, out-of-plane easy magnetization axis, and long spin relaxation time ${T}_{1}$ of ab…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Pivetta, M. Blanco-Rey, S. Reynaud, R. Baltic, A. Rary-Zinque, S. Toda Cosi, F. Patthey, B. V. Sorokin, A. Singha, F. Donati, A. Barla, L. Persichetti, P. Gambardella, A. Arnau, F. Delgado, S. Rusponi, and H. Brune

We report magnetic bistability in single Dy atoms on NaCl(100) thin films. Individual Dy atoms substituting Na at the surface of the NaCl layer are thermally stable up to at least 300 K, display 4f9 occupancy, out-of-plane easy magnetization axis, and long spin relaxation time T1 of about 10 s at 2.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-Atom Magnets on Thermally Stable Adsorption Sites: Dy on NaCl(100)

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin-Split Superconductivity in Spin-Orbit Coupled Hybrid Nanowires with Ferromagnetic Barriers
item.description:
Author(s): J. Zhao, A. Mazanik, D. Razmadze, Y. Liu, P. Krogstrup, F. S. Bergeret, and S. Vaitiekėnas

Tunneling spectroscopy on hybrid Josephson junctions (made of a ferromagnetic insulator, a semiconductor, and aluminum) reveals spin-split superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086302] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Zhao, A. Mazanik, D. Razmadze, Y. Liu, P. Krogstrup, F. S. Bergeret, and S. Vaitiekėnas

Tunneling spectroscopy on hybrid Josephson junctions (made of a ferromagnetic insulator, a semiconductor, and aluminum) reveals spin-split superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086302] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Split Superconductivity in Spin-Orbit Coupled Hybrid Nanowires with Ferromagnetic Barriers

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Kinetic Energy Driven Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu

We construct a minimal model of interacting fermions establishing a ferromagnetic insulating phase. It is based on the Hubbard model on a trimerized triangular lattice in the regime of $t≫|{t}^{′}|>0$ with $t$ and ${t}^{′}$ the intra- and intertrimer hopping amplitudes, respectively. At the $\fra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086504] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu

We construct a minimal model of interacting fermions establishing a ferromagnetic insulating phase. It is based on the Hubbard model on a trimerized triangular lattice in the regime of t≫|t′|>0 with t and t′ the intra- and intertrimer hopping amplitudes, respectively. At the 13-filling, each trim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086504] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Kinetic Energy Driven Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.dc:creator: Jinyuan Ye, Yuchi He, and Congjun Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fermion Mediated Pairing in the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida to Efimov Transition Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Geyue Cai, Henry Ando, Sarah McCusker, and Cheng Chin

The Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction and Efimov physics are two distinct quantum phenomena in condensed matter and nuclear physics, respectively. The RKKY interaction describes correlations between impurities mediated by an electron gas, while Efimov physics describes universal bound…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 083403] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Geyue Cai, Henry Ando, Sarah McCusker, and Cheng Chin

The Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction and Efimov physics are two distinct quantum phenomena in condensed matter and nuclear physics, respectively. The RKKY interaction describes correlations between impurities mediated by an electron gas, while Efimov physics describes universal bound…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 083403] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Fermion Mediated Pairing in the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida to Efimov Transition Regime
item.dc:creator: Geyue Cai, Henry Ando, Sarah McCusker, and Cheng Chin

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Fluxoid Solitons in Superconducting Tapered Tubes and Bottlenecks
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Kokkeler, Mateo Uldemolins, Francisco Lobo, F. Sebastian Bergeret, Elsa Prada, and Pablo San-Jose

A fluxoid soliton is a new type of topologically protected state that appears in thin-walled tubular superconductors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086001] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Kokkeler, Mateo Uldemolins, Francisco Lobo, F. Sebastian Bergeret, Elsa Prada, and Pablo San-Jose

A fluxoid soliton is a new type of topologically protected state that appears in thin-walled tubular superconductors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086001] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluxoid Solitons in Superconducting Tapered Tubes and Bottlenecks

item.title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng Chen, Wen O. Wang, Jia-Xin Zhang, Leon Balents, and D. N. Sheng

Moiré materials provide exciting platforms for studying the interplay of strong electronic correlation and large magnetic flux effects. We study the lightly doped Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a triangular lattice through the large-scale density matrix renormalization group and determinantal quantum M…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086503] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity in the Triangular-Lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard Model

item.title: Dynamics of Current-Induced Switching in the Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Alina Rupp, Daniel Rosenbach, Torsten Röper, Dominik Hoborka, Alexey A. Taskin, Yoichi Ando, and Erwann Bocquillon

Ferromagnetic topological insulators in the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) regime host chiral, dissipationless edge states whose propagation direction is determined by the internal magnetization. Under suitable conditions, a strong electrical bias can induce magnetization reversal, and thus flip the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086602] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alina Rupp, Daniel Rosenbach, Torsten Röper, Dominik Hoborka, Alexey A. Taskin, Yoichi Ando, and Erwann Bocquillon

Ferromagnetic topological insulators in the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) regime host chiral, dissipationless edge states whose propagation direction is determined by the internal magnetization. Under suitable conditions, a strong electrical bias can induce magnetization reversal, and thus flip the p…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086602] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.description:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

We demonstrate here that a chain of bilayer graphene quantum dots (BLGQDs) can realize topological quantum matter by effectively simulating a spin-1 chain that hosts the Haldane phase within a specific range of parameters. We describe a chain of BLGQDs with two electrons per dot using an atomistic t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086604] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots as a Quantum Simulator of Haldane Topological Quantum Matter
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miravet, Hassan Allami, Marek Korkusiński, and Paweł Hawrylak

item.title: Suppression of Thermal Conductivity via Singlet-Dominated Scattering in ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez

We measured the thermal conductivity of the rare-earth orthoferrites, $R{\mathrm{FeO}}_{3}$, where $R=\mathrm{Eu}$, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, and Yb from 3 to 300 K and see an anomalous strong suppression for ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$ over most of the temperature range. Using a Debye thermal transport mo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086705] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez

We measured the thermal conductivity of the rare-earth orthoferrites, RFeO3, where R=Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, and Yb from 3 to 300 K and see an anomalous strong suppression for TmFeO3 over most of the temperature range. Using a Debye thermal transport model, we demonstrate that this suppression i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086705] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Suppression of Thermal Conductivity via Singlet-Dominated Scattering in ${\mathrm{TmFeO}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: M. L. McLanahan, D. Lederman, and A. P. Ramirez
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light
item.description:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruiping Guo, Haowei Chen, Wenhui Duan, Yong Xu, and Chong Wang

Uniform optical illumination is predicted to induce static but spatially inhomogeneous charge redistribution within a supercell in moiré superlattices.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086903] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Manipulating Charge Distribution in Moiré Superlattices by Light

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: $q$-Gaussian Crossover in Overlap Spectra toward 3D Edwards-Anderson Criticality
item.description:
Author(s): Yaprak Önder, Abbas Ali Saberi, and Roderich Moessner

We introduce a spectral approach to characterizing the three-dimensional Edwards-Anderson spin glass. By analyzing the eigenvalue statistics of overlap matrices constructed from two-dimensional cross sections, we identify a crossover from the Wigner semicircle law at high temperatures toward a Gauss…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 087103] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yaprak Önder, Abbas Ali Saberi, and Roderich Moessner

We introduce a spectral approach to characterizing the three-dimensional Edwards-Anderson spin glass. By analyzing the eigenvalue statistics of overlap matrices constructed from two-dimensional cross sections, we identify a crossover from the Wigner semicircle law at high temperatures toward a Gauss…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 087103] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: $q$-Gaussian Crossover in Overlap Spectra toward 3D Edwards-Anderson Criticality

item.title: Stabilizer Rényi Entropy Encodes Fusion Rules of Topological Defects and Boundaries
item.description:
Author(s): Masahiro Hoshino and Yuto Ashida

We demonstrate that the stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE), a computable measure of quantum magic, can serve as an information-theoretic probe for universal properties associated with conformal defects in one-dimensional quantum critical systems. Using boundary conformal field theory, we show that open …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 080402] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masahiro Hoshino and Yuto Ashida

We demonstrate that the stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE), a computable measure of quantum magic, can serve as an information-theoretic probe for universal properties associated with conformal defects in one-dimensional quantum critical systems. Using boundary conformal field theory, we show that open …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 080402] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Stabilizer Rényi Entropy Encodes Fusion Rules of Topological Defects and Boundaries
item.dc:creator: Masahiro Hoshino and Yuto Ashida

item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Minimal Theory of Strange Carriers
item.description:
Author(s): Simone Fratini

I explore a theory of transport and optical properties of strange metallic carriers in strongly correlated systems that follows from assuming that the diffusion constant has reached its quantum limit $D=ℏ/m$, and that such quantum carriers behave as distinguishable particles as they would in an elec…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086301] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Simone Fratini

I explore a theory of transport and optical properties of strange metallic carriers in strongly correlated systems that follows from assuming that the diffusion constant has reached its quantum limit D=ℏ/m, and that such quantum carriers behave as distinguishable particles as they would in an electr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086301] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Van Hove Singularities, Superconductivity, and the Josephson Diode Effect in ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter

Superconductivity in the transition-metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$ has been attributed to the proximity of a three-dimensional Van Hove singularity to the Fermi level. In isostructural ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$, recently used as the weak link in a Josephson diode, a similar Van Hove singular…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086401] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter

Superconductivity in the transition-metal dichalcogenide PdTe2 has been attributed to the proximity of a three-dimensional Van Hove singularity to the Fermi level. In isostructural NiTe2, recently used as the weak link in a Josephson diode, a similar Van Hove singularity has been predicted to occur,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086401] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Van Hove Singularities, Superconductivity, and the Josephson Diode Effect in ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Emily C. McFarlane, Antonio Sanna, Matthew J. Gilbert, Jonas A. Krieger, Mihir Date, Gabriele Domaine, Banabir Pal, Anirban Chakraborty, Pranava K. Sivakumar, Procopios C. Constantinou, Anna Hartl, Enrico G. Della Valle, Camilla Pellegrini, Vladimir N. Strocov, Stuart S. P. Parkin, and Niels B. M. Schröter

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Laser-Driven Structural Transformation from a Bulk Crystal to a Layered Material
item.description:
Author(s): Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Ofer Neufeld, and Peng Chen

Laser-induced phase transitions offer pathways of phase transitions that are inaccessible by conventional stimuli. In this Letter, we conduct ab initio simulations to numerically demonstrate a novel laser-induced structural transformation: converting a bulk crystal into a layered van der Waals mater…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086902] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Ofer Neufeld, and Peng Chen

Laser-induced phase transitions offer pathways of phase transitions that are inaccessible by conventional stimuli. In this Letter, we conduct ab initio simulations to numerically demonstrate a novel laser-induced structural transformation: converting a bulk crystal into a layered van der Waals mater…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 086902] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Laser-Driven Structural Transformation from a Bulk Crystal to a Layered Material
item.dc:creator: Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Ofer Neufeld, and Peng Chen

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Transport in Interacting Spin Chains: Exact Derivation of the Tracy-Widom Distribution
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuya Fujimoto and Tomohiro Sasamoto

We theoretically study quantum spin transport in a one-dimensional folded XXZ model with an alternating domain-wall initial state via the Bethe ansatz technique, exactly demonstrating that a probability distribution of finding a leftmost up spin with an appropriate scaling variable converges to the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 087102] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kazuya Fujimoto and Tomohiro Sasamoto

We theoretically study quantum spin transport in a one-dimensional folded XXZ model with an alternating domain-wall initial state via the Bethe ansatz technique, exactly demonstrating that a probability distribution of finding a leftmost up spin with an appropriate scaling variable converges to the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 087102] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Transport in Interacting Spin Chains: Exact Derivation of the Tracy-Widom Distribution

item.title: XY Model with Persistent Noise
item.description:
Author(s): Xia-qing Shi, Hugues Chaté, and Benoît Mahault

We consider a 2D XY model subjected to time-correlated noise, a model of direct relevance to active crystals, which were shown recently to be able to support very large deformations without melting in the presence of persistent fluctuations. We find that our persistent XY model can remain quasiorder…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 088302] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xia-qing Shi, Hugues Chaté, and Benoît Mahault

We consider a 2D XY model subjected to time-correlated noise, a model of direct relevance to active crystals, which were shown recently to be able to support very large deformations without melting in the presence of persistent fluctuations. We find that our persistent XY model can remain quasiorder…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 088302] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: XY Model with Persistent Noise
item.dc:creator: Xia-qing Shi, Hugues Chaté, and Benoît Mahault
